title: Court managers are India’s underused judicial reform
description: India’s judicial reform debate usually returns to a familiar demand: appoint more judges. The demand is justified. Courts are overburdened and district courts carry the heaviest pressure of ordinary justice. But the debate often misses another question: how much judicial time is lost to administrative work that should not require a judge’s direct attention?A judge’s core function is adjudication. Yet, the working life of a court also depends on scheduling, records, staff coordination, digital sy
